steps.title: Find the tense signal already in the sentence
steps.content:
The sentence gives a parallel example before the blank: "the exiled character of Bình connects to his native Saigon through the food he prepares." Both "connects" and "prepares" are simple present tense. The blank must use the same tense to describe what Linda does in Bitter in the Mouth.
  • Choice A — experienced: Simple past — mismatches the present-tense pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — had experienced: Past perfect — mismatches the present-tense pattern. ✗
  • Choice C — experiences: Simple present — matches "connects" and "prepares." ✓
  • Choice D — will be experiencing: Future progressive — mismatches the present-tense pattern. ✗

steps.title: Confirm tense consistency
steps.content: Literary present tense is standard when describing what characters do in novels. The passage establishes this tense with "connects" and "prepares," so the blank must also be simple present. "Experiences" is the only option that fits. That matches Choice C — experiences.
